meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first app.
content.answer_block: Building a SaaS pricing model is the fastest way to signal engineering maturity to employers. By implementing tiered subscription logic, Stripe integration, and usage-based billing, you transform a simple student project into a professional, revenue-ready application that proves you can handle complex business logic and secure payment flows.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the ultimate career bottleneck. You have the skills, but without a project that demonstrates real-world complexity, you remain invisible to hiring managers. The difference between a 'tutorial app' and a 'SaaS product' isn't just code—it is the ability to monetize value.

Today, we stop building toy projects. By integrating a professional SaaS pricing engine, you move from writing functions to engineering business infrastructure. This is the exact moment your resume shifts from 'student' to 'software engineer.'

steps.title: Define Your Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Establish a clear value proposition for your tiers (e.g., Free, Pro, Enterprise). This defines your database schema requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a SaaS platform with three pricing tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for monthly_price, feature_limits, and user_role permissions.

steps.title: Stripe API Integration
steps.description: Connect your backend to Stripe to handle subscription states. This is the industry standard for payment processing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Node.js service using the Stripe SDK to create a subscription checkout session for a specific price ID.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-dependency stripe && claude generate-file stripe_service.js

steps.title: Implement Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Handle asynchronous payment events like 'invoice.paid' or 'customer.subscription.deleted' to sync your database with Stripe.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write an Express.js webhook route to verify Stripe signatures and update the 'is_pro' status in the PostgreSQL database.

steps.title: Frontend Pricing Table
steps.description: Build a responsive UI that displays your tiers and triggers the checkout process for your users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a React pricing component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the 'Pro' plan and calls the checkout API on click.

steps.title: Middleware Protection
steps.description: Secure your premium features by checking the user's subscription status before rendering sensitive routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication middleware that checks if the current user has an active subscription before granting access to premium routes.

pro_tips: Always implement a 'cancel at period end' feature; it reduces friction and increases user trust significantly.
Use environment variables for all Stripe keys; never hardcode secrets, even in a student project.
Add a 'free trial' flag to your database; it allows for better A/B testing and marketing data.
Log every Stripe event in a local audit table; debugging payment failures is impossible without a local transaction history.
Focus on the 'billing portal' URL provided by Stripe; it saves you from building complex user-facing subscription management UIs.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to Vercel or Railway, add a 'Powered by [Your Name]' footer, and link the live demo directly on your LinkedIn profile.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical foundation to build a project that stands out. Don't just code—build, deploy, and scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
